corpus.boundary: Matched case-insensitively on 'ched-pp' OR 'chedpp' anywhere in the trace Title. Because 'ched-pp' does not substring-match 'ched-p', no exclusion filter was needed or used (one would have wrongly discarded the entire target set). The excluded single-p CHED-P (products of animal origin) family totals 96 traces and reconciles exactly: 161 CHED-P-family title matches minus the 65 CHED-PP selected. Those 96 break down as 60 under notification/ched-p/, 30 shared document/ tests titled '› CHED-P ›', 4 under smoke/smoke-ched-p-notifier-inspector-decision-flows.spec.ts, and 2 titled '› CHEDP:'. Directory segments notification/ched-p/ and notification/ched-pp/ were compared as full path segments, not substrings.
findings: No accessibility/ traces exist in the corpus at all — zero hits for 'accessibility/' across all 383 titles, for any CHED type. The accessibility/ched-pp-* traces anticipated by the task brief do not exist. CHED-PP accessibility surface is UNCOVERED — a finding, not an absence of requirement.
32 of the 65 (49%) live OUTSIDE notification/ched-pp/ — in smoke/ and shared, CHED-type-parameterised document/ specs. Restricting to the dedicated directory would have lost roughly half the corpus.

Single-source features (one trace each, so no corroboration available): CSV bulk commodity entry (9a918b86), Common User Charge + the SOAP retrieval boundary (a61cf55b), and HMI check auto-completion (e10b7cd5). Treat requirements mined from these as unreplicated.

The 3 cloning traces are the thinnest in the corpus (48-50 actions) yet ebd5a5d2 spans 16 pages — the clone prefill does the work the notifier would otherwise do by hand, so these traces under-represent the field set rather than the feature.

Duplicate retries are common: 8 scenarios were recorded 2-3 times (smoke :21/:34/:67, copy-as-new :15/:56, cloning :10, notification :141/:264). Within each duplicate group the rationale names the best-captured run by page count; the others are corroboration only.
corpus.classificationNote: core-journey vs post-submission is a genuine judgement call for the 10 smoke/ traces and the 6 valid-inspector document traces: each CREATES a CHED-PP end to end AND THEN exercises an inspector decision, so both enum definitions fit. They are filed here as post-submission because the spec is named 'notifier-inspector-decision-flows' and each test's assertion lands on the decision outcome ('should validate ROW CHED-PP AFTER B2C notifier submission'). Every such rationale flags the full-submission prologue explicitly, so the next wave can mine them for creation-form requirements regardless of bucket. A prior index at this path filed the smoke traces as core-journey instead — the disagreement is about labelling, not about the evidence.

traces.title: B2C Importer (Not Agent): Submits Valid CHEDPP Notification which can be overridden
traces.testName: CHED-PP notification › B2C Importer (Not Agent): Submits Valid CHEDPP Notification which can be overridden
traces.rationale: Canonical happy-path B2C submission across 17 pages with zero errors — the reference end-to-end CHED-PP creation trace. Start here.

traces.title: Autoclear EU CHEDPP
traces.testName: CHED-PP notification › Autoclear EU CHEDPP
traces.rationale: Full EU CHED-PP creation through to automatic clearance; longest trace in the corpus (5m), so covers the widest slice of the submit-to-cleared lifecycle.

traces.title: should be able to view notification details on the review page
traces.testName: CHED-PP manipulation › should be able to view notification details on the review page
traces.rationale: Builds a notification end to end then renders the review/check-answers page — best single source for the complete captured field set in one snapshot.

traces.title: should be able to amend notification
traces.testName: CHED-PP manipulation › should be able to amend notification
traces.rationale: Creates a notification end to end then re-enters the form to amend it — evidence for the edit / change-answer path.

traces.title: should be able to delete notification
traces.testName: CHED-PP manipulation › should be able to delete notification
traces.rationale: Creates a notification end to end then deletes it — covers the draft lifecycle terminus.

traces.title: Submit CHED-PP Notification with Test and Trial Information and Confirm Data in Dynamics and Trade Platform
traces.testName: CHED-PP EU Harmonisation › Submit CHED-PP Notification with Test and Trial Information and Confirm Data in Dynamics and Trade Platform
traces.rationale: Full submission carrying test-and-trial purpose data, then asserts the downstream Dynamics / Trade Platform payload — ties UI fields to the outbound contract.

traces.title: Submit CHED-PP Notification with Machinery EPPO Code and Confirm Data in Dynamics and Trade Platform
traces.testName: CHED-PP EU Harmonisation › Submit CHED-PP Notification with Machinery EPPO Code and Confirm Data in Dynamics and Trade Platform
traces.rationale: Full submission for the machinery / EPPO-code commodity shape plus downstream payload assertions — a distinct commodity taxonomy from the standard plant path.

traces.title: CHED-PP Arrival to BCP Date in the future
traces.testName: CHED-PP Notifications Date validations › CHED-PP Arrival to BCP Date in the future
traces.rationale: Arrival-to-BCP date boundary rules; highest action count in the whole corpus (287) across 13 pages, so likely sweeps many date permutations and their messages. Richest validation trace available.

traces.title: CHED-PP Arrival to BCP Date in the past
traces.testName: CHED-PP Notifications Date validations › CHED-PP Arrival to BCP Date in the past
traces.rationale: Past-dated arrival to BCP — the negative half of the date rule; prime source for date error copy.

traces.title: B2C notifier cannot upload an invalid TXT commercial invoice attachment
traces.testName: Invalid commercial invoice attachment upload › CHED-PP › B2C notifier cannot upload an invalid TXT commercial invoice attachment
traces.rationale: Plain-text rejection. Notable that TXT is disallowed while DOC/XLS are allowed — a real, non-obvious rule to carry forward. 7 pages, best-captured of the invalid-invoice set.

traces.title: B2C notifier cannot upload an invalid EMPTY commercial invoice attachment
traces.testName: Invalid commercial invoice attachment upload › CHED-PP › B2C notifier cannot upload an invalid EMPTY commercial invoice attachment
traces.rationale: Zero-byte commercial invoice rejection — error state for the empty-file rule.

traces.title: B2C notifier cannot upload an invalid LARGE commercial invoice attachment
traces.testName: Invalid commercial invoice attachment upload › CHED-PP › B2C notifier cannot upload an invalid LARGE commercial invoice attachment
traces.rationale: Oversize commercial invoice rejection — should pin the file-size ceiling and its message.

traces.title: B2C notifier cannot upload an invalid ZIP commercial invoice attachment
traces.testName: Invalid commercial invoice attachment upload › CHED-PP › B2C notifier cannot upload an invalid ZIP commercial invoice attachment
traces.rationale: Disallowed archive type rejection — defines the negative edge of the accepted-types list.

traces.title: B2B inspector cannot upload an invalid TXT attachment
traces.testName: Invalid inspector attachment upload › CHED-PP › B2B inspector cannot upload an invalid TXT attachment
traces.rationale: Plain-text rejection on the inspector surface; 9 pages, the most contextful of the invalid-inspector set.

traces.title: B2B inspector cannot upload an invalid LARGE attachment
traces.testName: Invalid inspector attachment upload › CHED-PP › B2B inspector cannot upload an invalid LARGE attachment
traces.rationale: Oversize rejection on the inspector upload surface — corroborates that the size rule applies to both roles.

traces.title: B2B inspector cannot upload an invalid ZIP attachment
traces.testName: Invalid inspector attachment upload › CHED-PP › B2B inspector cannot upload an invalid ZIP attachment
traces.rationale: Archive rejection on the inspector surface — mirrors the notifier-side ZIP rule.

traces.title: B2B inspector cannot upload an invalid EMPTY attachment
traces.testName: Invalid inspector attachment upload › CHED-PP › B2B inspector cannot upload an invalid EMPTY attachment
traces.rationale: Zero-byte rejection on the inspector surface.

traces.title: Split consignment shows valid and rejected tabs when commodities have mixed decisions
traces.testName: CHED-PP notification › Split consignment shows valid and rejected tabs when commodities have mixed decisions
traces.rationale: Split-consignment tabbed UI under mixed per-commodity decisions; 14 pages and error-free — the cleanest split trace to mine.

traces.title: Split consignment: Hold changed to Non compliant creates Rejected <ref>R child CHEDs after valid ched is generated
traces.testName: CHED-PP notification › Split consignment: Hold changed to Non compliant creates Rejected <ref>R child CHEDs after valid ched is generated
traces.rationale: Hold-to-non-compliant transition minting <ref>R child CHEDs; the only error-free trace at this line, so the reference for child-CHED reference numbering.

traces.title: Split consignment: Hold changed to Non compliant creates Rejected <ref>R child CHEDs after valid ched is generated on partial checks
traces.testName: CHED-PP notification › Split consignment: Hold changed to Non compliant creates Rejected <ref>R child CHEDs after valid ched is generated on partial checks
traces.rationale: Partial-checks variant of the hold/non-compliant split rule, at 260 actions — the fullest run of this scenario. Carries 1 error so may also surface state-transition messaging.

traces.title: Split consignment: Hold changed to Non compliant creates Rejected <ref>R child CHEDs after valid ched is generated on partial checks
traces.testName: CHED-PP notification › Split consignment: Hold changed to Non compliant creates Rejected <ref>R child CHEDs after valid ched is generated on partial checks
traces.rationale: Retry of the :141 partial-checks split case; duplicate of a67b1298 — corroboration only.

traces.title: Split consignment: Hold changed to Non compliant creates Rejected <ref>R child CHEDs after valid ched is generated on partial checks
traces.testName: CHED-PP notification › Split consignment: Hold changed to Non compliant creates Rejected <ref>R child CHEDs after valid ched is generated on partial checks
traces.rationale: Third run of the :141 partial-checks split case; shortest of the three — prefer a67b1298.

traces.title: Split consignment: Valid and Rejected cheds are generated when commodities have checks updated to compliant and non compliant
traces.testName: CHED-PP notification › Split consignment: Valid and Rejected cheds are generated when commodities have checks updated to compliant and non compliant
traces.rationale: Compliant/non-compliant commodity mix producing paired Valid and Rejected CHEDs — the split-generation rule. 4 pages, best-captured of the three :264 runs.

traces.title: Split consignment: Valid and Rejected cheds are generated when commodities have checks updated to compliant and non compliant
traces.testName: CHED-PP notification › Split consignment: Valid and Rejected cheds are generated when commodities have checks updated to compliant and non compliant
traces.rationale: Retry of the :264 split-generation case; duplicate of fa10e9bf.

traces.title: Split consignment: Valid and Rejected cheds are generated when commodities have checks updated to compliant and non compliant
traces.testName: CHED-PP notification › Split consignment: Valid and Rejected cheds are generated when commodities have checks updated to compliant and non compliant
traces.rationale: Third run of the :264 split-generation case; duplicate of fa10e9bf.

traces.title: should validate copied CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission and B2B inspection
traces.testName: CHED-PP copy as new flows › should validate copied CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission and B2B inspection
traces.rationale: Copy-as-new prefill then submit and inspect — defines which fields carry over to a copy. 14 pages, by far the richest of the three :15 runs.

traces.title: should validate copied CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission and B2B inspection
traces.testName: CHED-PP copy as new flows › should validate copied CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission and B2B inspection
traces.rationale: Retry of the copy-as-new B2C case; only 2 pages captured — prefer ea99a3b5.

traces.title: should validate copied CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission and B2B inspection
traces.testName: CHED-PP copy as new flows › should validate copied CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission and B2B inspection
traces.rationale: Third run of the copy-as-new B2C case; duplicate of ba928f75.

traces.title: should copy ROW CHED-PP as new and validate after B2B inspection
traces.testName: CHED-PP copy as new flows › should copy ROW CHED-PP as new and validate after B2B inspection
traces.rationale: Copy-as-new on the ROW (rest-of-world) origin variant — ROW and EU differ in required fields. 5 pages, best-captured of the three :56 runs.

traces.title: should copy ROW CHED-PP as new and validate after B2B inspection
traces.testName: CHED-PP copy as new flows › should copy ROW CHED-PP as new and validate after B2B inspection
traces.rationale: Retry of the copy-as-new ROW case; duplicate of 84dfe524.

traces.title: should copy ROW CHED-PP as new and validate after B2B inspection
traces.testName: CHED-PP copy as new flows › should copy ROW CHED-PP as new and validate after B2B inspection
traces.rationale: Third run of the copy-as-new ROW case; duplicate of 84dfe524.

traces.title: B2C Importer Submits Valid Cloned CHEDPP Notification
traces.testName: Cloned CHED-PP notification › B2C Importer Submits Valid Cloned CHEDPP Notification
traces.rationale: Clone-then-submit; only 48 actions but 16 pages — the clone prefill does the work, which is itself the evidence about what cloning carries. Much the best of the three cloning runs.

traces.title: B2C Importer Submits Valid Cloned CHEDPP Notification
traces.testName: Cloned CHED-PP notification › B2C Importer Submits Valid Cloned CHEDPP Notification
traces.rationale: Retry of the cloning case; single page captured, so very thin — prefer ebd5a5d2.

traces.title: B2C Importer Submits Valid Cloned CHEDPP Notification
traces.testName: Cloned CHED-PP notification › B2C Importer Submits Valid Cloned CHEDPP Notification
traces.rationale: Third run of the cloning case; single page, thin — prefer ebd5a5d2.

traces.title: B2C Importer (Not Agent): Submits Valid CHEDPP Notification Using Article 72 Country and Commodities
traces.testName: Article 72 CHED-PP flows › B2C Importer (Not Agent): Submits Valid CHEDPP Notification Using Article 72 Country and Commodities
traces.rationale: Article 72 positive case (both country AND commodities in scope) — the reduced-checks regulatory path. Entry point to the 4-trace eligibility matrix.

traces.title: Submits New CHEDPP Notification Using Non-Article 72 Country and Commodities
traces.testName: Article 72 CHED-PP flows › Submits New CHEDPP Notification Using Non-Article 72 Country and Commodities
traces.rationale: Article 72 negative control (neither country nor commodities qualify) — the baseline the other three matrix cells are measured against. 15 pages.

traces.title: Submits New CHEDPP Notification Using Non-Article-72 Country and Article-72 Commodities
traces.testName: Article 72 CHED-PP flows › Submits New CHEDPP Notification Using Non-Article-72 Country and Article-72 Commodities
traces.rationale: Article 72 mixed cell — commodities qualify but country does not; pins the AND-semantics of the eligibility rule.

traces.title: Submits New CHEDPP Notification Using Article-72 Country and Non-Article-72 Commodities
traces.testName: Article 72 CHED-PP flows › Submits New CHEDPP Notification Using Article-72 Country and Non-Article-72 Commodities
traces.rationale: Article 72 mixed cell — country qualifies but commodities do not; the complementary half of the eligibility matrix.

traces.title: B2C Importer (Agent): Submits Valid CHEDPP Notification via CSV
traces.testName: CHED-PP notification › B2C Importer (Agent): Submits Valid CHEDPP Notification via CSV
traces.rationale: Bulk commodity entry via CSV upload rather than the form. ONLY CSV trace in the CHED-PP corpus — single-source evidence for that entire feature, so mine exhaustively.

traces.title: B2C notifier submits CUC CHEDPP → notification is NEW and SOAP returns the record
traces.testName: CHED-PP Common User Charge › B2C notifier submits CUC CHEDPP → notification is NEW and SOAP returns the record
traces.rationale: Common User Charge submission asserted through the SOAP retrieval boundary. ONLY CUC trace, and the sole evidence of the legacy SOAP interface — mine exhaustively.

traces.title: B2B Inspector (Not Agent): Submits Invalid EU CHEDPP Notification
traces.testName: CHED-PP notifier flows with inspector decisions › B2B Inspector (Not Agent): Submits Invalid EU CHEDPP Notification
traces.rationale: Inspector records a not-acceptable decision on an EU CHED-PP; 17 pages, error-free — richest post-submission trace. Contains a full submission prologue, so also strong core-journey evidence. NOTE: 'Invalid' reads as the decision outcome (counterpart to the sibling 'validate' tests), not a form-validation failure — confirm when opened.

traces.title: should hold checks and request amendment on EU CHED-PP after B2B notifier submission
traces.testName: CHED-PP notifier flows with inspector decisions › should hold checks and request amendment on EU CHED-PP after B2B notifier submission
traces.rationale: Inspector holds checks and requests amendment, driving the notification back to the notifier — the round-trip rework loop. Error-free at 199 actions; includes a full B2B submission prologue.

traces.title: should hold checks and request amendment on EU CHED-PP after B2B notifier submission
traces.testName: CHED-PP notifier flows with inspector decisions › should hold checks and request amendment on EU CHED-PP after B2B notifier submission
traces.rationale: Retry of the hold/request-amendment case; duplicate of 1f5b6eef, which is error-free and preferable.

traces.title: should validate EU CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission
traces.testName: CHED-PP notifier flows with inspector decisions › should validate EU CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission
traces.rationale: Inspector validates an EU-origin CHED-PP after a B2C submission; error-free full flow. Only EU+B2C decision trace; includes a full submission prologue.

traces.title: should validate ROW CHED-PP after B2B notifier submission
traces.testName: CHED-PP notifier flows with inspector decisions › should validate ROW CHED-PP after B2B notifier submission
traces.rationale: Inspector validates a ROW CHED-PP after B2B submission; 20 pages — the HIGHEST page count in the entire CHED-PP corpus, so the widest snapshot coverage anywhere. Includes a full B2B submission prologue; strong core-journey evidence too.

traces.title: should validate ROW CHED-PP after B2B notifier submission
traces.testName: CHED-PP notifier flows with inspector decisions › should validate ROW CHED-PP after B2B notifier submission
traces.rationale: Retry of the ROW B2B validate case; duplicate of f23a29f6.

traces.title: should validate ROW CHED-PP after B2B notifier submission
traces.testName: CHED-PP notifier flows with inspector decisions › should validate ROW CHED-PP after B2B notifier submission
traces.rationale: Third run of the ROW B2B validate case; single page captured — prefer f23a29f6.

traces.title: should validate ROW CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission
traces.testName: CHED-PP notifier flows with inspector decisions › should validate ROW CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission
traces.rationale: Inspector validates a ROW-origin CHED-PP after B2C submission; 19 pages, best-captured of the three :21 runs and second-widest in the corpus. Includes a full B2C submission prologue.

traces.title: should validate ROW CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission
traces.testName: CHED-PP notifier flows with inspector decisions › should validate ROW CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission
traces.rationale: Retry of the ROW B2C validate case; duplicate of b1982c74.

traces.title: should validate ROW CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission
traces.testName: CHED-PP notifier flows with inspector decisions › should validate ROW CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission
traces.rationale: Third run of the ROW B2C validate case; duplicate of b1982c74.

traces.title: should auto-complete the HMI check outcome when risk decision is no inspection
traces.testName: [IMTA-20591] CHED-PP HMI auto-completion › should auto-complete the HMI check outcome when risk decision is no inspection
traces.rationale: HMI (horticultural marketing inspection) check outcome auto-completes when the risk engine returns no-inspection — a decision-side automation rule. ONLY HMI trace; carries a Jira ref (IMTA-20591) worth chasing.

traces.title: CHEDPP: notifier and inspector can view attached documents after permit
traces.testName: Document access › CHEDPP: notifier and inspector can view attached documents after permit
traces.rationale: Cross-role document visibility once the CHED-PP is permitted — an authorisation rule that outlives the decision.

traces.title: CHEDPP: notifier and inspector can view attached documents after rejection
traces.testName: Document access after rejection › CHEDPP: notifier and inspector can view attached documents after rejection
traces.rationale: Cross-role document visibility after rejection — complement of document-access, confirming access is not revoked on a negative decision.

traces.title: B2B inspector uploads a valid DOCX attachment
traces.testName: Valid inspector attachment upload › CHED-PP › B2B inspector uploads a valid DOCX attachment
traces.rationale: Inspector-side attachment upload, DOCX accepted — one cell of the inspector accepted-file-type matrix. 8 pages.

traces.title: B2B inspector uploads a valid PNG attachment
traces.testName: Valid inspector attachment upload › CHED-PP › B2B inspector uploads a valid PNG attachment
traces.rationale: Inspector-side attachment upload, PNG accepted; 11 pages — richest snapshot coverage of the valid-inspector set, so the one to open if only one is opened.
